:PROPERTIES:

#+begin_src emacs-lisp
;;; gptel-tools.el --- Tool definitions for gptel  -*- lexical-binding: t; -*-
;;
;; AUTO-GENERATED from gptel-tools.org -- do not edit directly.
;; Edit the .org file and tangle with C-c C-v t.

(require 'gptel)
(require 'org)
(require 'org-element)
#+end_src

* Emacs / buffer tools
** current_buffer
Return the contents of the most recent non-chat buffer the user was working in.

#+begin_src emacs-lisp
(gptel-make-tool
 :name "current_buffer"
 :function (lambda ()
             (with-current-buffer (other-buffer (current-buffer) t)
               (buffer-substring-no-properties (point-min) (point-max))))
 :description "Return the contents of the user's current buffer (not the gptel chat buffer)."
 :args nil
 :category "emacs")
#+end_src

** list_buffers
List visible (non-internal) buffer names.

#+begin_src emacs-lisp
(gptel-make-tool
 :name "list_buffers"
 :function (lambda ()
             (mapconcat #'buffer-name
                        (seq-filter (lambda (b)
                                      (not (string-prefix-p " " (buffer-name b))))
                                    (buffer-list))
                        "\n"))
 :description "List all visible buffer names."
 :args nil
 :category "emacs")
#+end_src

* Filesystem tools
** read_file
#+begin_src emacs-lisp
(gptel-make-tool
 :name "read_file"
 :function (lambda (path)
             (with-temp-buffer
               (insert-file-contents (expand-file-name path))
               (buffer-string)))
 :description "Read and return the full contents of a file."
 :args (list '(:name "path"
               :type string
               :description "Absolute or ~-expanded path to the file."))
 :category "filesystem")
#+end_src

** list_directory
#+begin_src emacs-lisp
(gptel-make-tool
 :name "list_directory"
 :function (lambda (path)
             (mapconcat #'identity
                        (directory-files (expand-file-name path) nil "^[^.]")
                        "\n"))
 :description "List non-hidden files and directories in a directory."
 :args (list '(:name "path"
               :type string
               :description "Directory path."))
 :category "filesystem")
#+end_src

* Org-mode tools
** Helper: resolve Org buffer
Used by all Org tools to accept a file path, buffer name, or empty string.

#+begin_src emacs-lisp
(defun my/gptel--resolve-org-buffer (target)
  "Resolve TARGET to a live Org buffer.
TARGET may be a file path, a buffer name, or nil (= current buffer)."
  (cond
   ((or (null target) (string-empty-p target))
    (current-buffer))
   ((get-buffer target))
   ((file-exists-p (expand-file-name target))
    (find-file-noselect (expand-file-name target)))
   (t (error "Cannot resolve Org target: %s" target))))
#+end_src

** org_current_subtree
Read the subtree at point in the user's last-visited Org buffer.

#+begin_src emacs-lisp
(gptel-make-tool
 :name "org_current_subtree"
 :function (lambda ()
             (with-current-buffer (other-buffer (current-buffer) t)
               (unless (derived-mode-p 'org-mode)
                 (error "Current buffer is not in org-mode"))
               (save-excursion
                 (org-back-to-heading t)
                 (buffer-substring-no-properties
                  (point)
                  (progn (org-end-of-subtree t t) (point))))))
 :description "Return the full text of the Org subtree at point in the user's current buffer (including the heading and all children)."
 :args nil
 :category "org")
#+end_src

** org_list_headings
#+begin_src emacs-lisp
(gptel-make-tool
 :name "org_list_headings"
 :function (lambda (target)
             (with-current-buffer (my/gptel--resolve-org-buffer target)
               (org-with-wide-buffer
                (goto-char (point-min))
                (let (headings)
                  (org-map-entries
                   (lambda ()
                     (push (format "%s %s"
                                   (make-string (org-current-level) ?*)
                                   (org-get-heading t t t t))
                           headings)))
                  (mapconcat #'identity (nreverse headings) "\n")))))
 :description "List all headings (with their level) in an Org file or buffer."
 :args (list '(:name "target"
               :type string
               :description "File path, buffer name, or empty string for the user's current buffer."))
 :category "org")
#+end_src

** org_read_heading
#+begin_src emacs-lisp
(gptel-make-tool
 :name "org_read_heading"
 :function (lambda (target heading)
             (with-current-buffer (my/gptel--resolve-org-buffer target)
               (org-with-wide-buffer
                (goto-char (point-min))
                (if (re-search-forward
                     (format org-complex-heading-regexp-format
                             (regexp-quote heading))
                     nil t)
                    (progn
                      (org-back-to-heading t)
                      (buffer-substring-no-properties
                       (point)
                       (progn (org-end-of-subtree t t) (point))))
                  (error "Heading not found: %s" heading)))))
 :description "Return the full subtree (heading + body + children) of the first heading matching HEADING in TARGET."
 :args (list '(:name "target"
               :type string
               :description "File path, buffer name, or empty string for current buffer.")
             '(:name "heading"
               :type string
               :description "Exact heading text to look up (without leading asterisks)."))
 :category "org")
#+end_src

** org_insert_under_heading
Write tool — prompts for confirmation before inserting.

#+begin_src emacs-lisp
(gptel-make-tool
 :name "org_insert_under_heading"
 :function (lambda (target heading content)
             (with-current-buffer (my/gptel--resolve-org-buffer target)
               (org-with-wide-buffer
                (goto-char (point-min))
                (if (re-search-forward
                     (format org-complex-heading-regexp-format
                             (regexp-quote heading))
                     nil t)
                    (progn
                      (org-back-to-heading t)
                      (org-end-of-meta-data t)
                      (insert content)
                      (unless (bolp) (insert "\n"))
                      (format "Inserted %d chars under %S in %s"
                              (length content) heading (buffer-name)))
                  (error "Heading not found: %s" heading)))))
 :description "Insert CONTENT under the first heading matching HEADING in TARGET (after any property drawer)."
 :args (list '(:name "target"
               :type string
               :description "File path, buffer name, or empty string for current buffer.")
             '(:name "heading"
               :type string
               :description "Exact heading text.")
             '(:name "content"
               :type string
               :description "Text to insert. Include Org markup as needed."))
 :category "org"
 :confirm t)
#+end_src

** org_insert_src_block
#+begin_src emacs-lisp
(gptel-make-tool
 :name "org_insert_src_block"
 :function (lambda (target heading language code)
             (with-current-buffer (my/gptel--resolve-org-buffer target)
               (org-with-wide-buffer
                (goto-char (point-min))
                (if (re-search-forward
                     (format org-complex-heading-regexp-format
                             (regexp-quote heading))
                     nil t)
                    (progn
                      (org-back-to-heading t)
                      (org-end-of-meta-data t)
                      (insert (format "#+begin_src %s\n%s\n#+end_src\n"
                                      language
                                      (string-trim-right code)))
                      (format "Inserted %s src block under %S" language heading))
                  (error "Heading not found: %s" heading)))))
 :description "Insert an Org source block (#+begin_src LANGUAGE … #+end_src) under HEADING in TARGET."
 :args (list '(:name "target"
               :type string
               :description "File path, buffer name, or empty string for current buffer.")
             '(:name "heading"
               :type string
               :description "Exact heading text.")
             '(:name "language"
               :type string
               :description "Source block language, e.g. \"emacs-lisp\", \"python\", \"shell\".")
             '(:name "code"
               :type string
               :description "Code to put inside the block."))
 :category "org"
 :confirm t)
#+end_src

** org_search
Uses ripgrep if available, falls back to grep.

#+begin_src emacs-lisp
(gptel-make-tool
 :name "org_search"
 :function (lambda (query directory)
             (let ((dir (expand-file-name (if (string-empty-p directory)
                                              org-directory
                                            directory))))
               (with-temp-buffer
                 (let ((default-directory dir))
                   (if (executable-find "rg")
                       (call-process "rg" nil t nil
                                     "--no-heading" "--line-number"
                                     "--glob" "*.org" query)
                     (call-process "grep" nil t nil
                                   "-rn" "--include=*.org" query ".")))
                 (buffer-string))))
 :description "Search Org files for QUERY (plain string). Returns matching file:line:text rows."
 :args (list '(:name "query"
               :type string
               :description "Search string (literal, not regex unless your tool supports it).")
             '(:name "directory"
               :type string
               :description "Directory to search. Empty string uses `org-directory'."))
 :category "org")
#+end_src

* Shell tools
:PROPERTIES:
:CUSTOM_ID: shell
:END:

⚠ These execute arbitrary commands. Each has =:confirm t=.

** run_shell_command
#+begin_src emacs-lisp
(gptel-make-tool
 :name "run_shell_command"
 :function (lambda (command)
             (shell-command-to-string command))
 :description "Run a shell command and return its stdout. Use with caution."
 :args (list '(:name "command"
               :type string
               :description "Shell command to execute."))
 :category "shell"
 :confirm t)
#+end_src

* Footer
